considered: Whether the recurring 'eval pipeline unavailable in worktrees' issue (2/2 runs now) should rise to framework-level outcome B (roadmap edit adding an explicit 'fix eval in worktrees' V1.x checklist item)
decision_reason: Outcome A — bar for B not cleared on bounded-and-falsifiable. The issue is real and recurring (2/2 runs cannot run eval in worktrees because .venv is absent and VOYAGE_API_KEY is not exported into worktree env), but the fix is non-trivial: worktrees inherit env from the spawning shell, the scheduled-task bin/blindspot wrapper sources ~/.blindspot/.env (commit 1f34f30) but worktrees don't run through that wrapper. The fix is mid-complexity (export env from orchestrator to subagent dispatch, or have subagents source the env file explicitly), not a 1-2 file roadmap edit. Keeping outcome A this run; a future run with more data and a concrete fix proposal can clear the bar.

summary: Tightened the 'Known blind spots OF this community' section in community_profiles/vc-blogosphere.md to 5 concrete, schema-aligned bullets.
rationale: vc-blogosphere was the smallest profile in the set (3473 bytes) and weakest on _schema.md rigor — prior blind-spot bullets like 'Pro-investor / pro-founder by default' pointed in the right direction but didn't pin down a concrete failure mode the Risk Officer can cross-check. Replaced with 5 mechanism-specific bullets: W-2 grantee tax-timing gap, YC/power-law pattern-matching that invisibilizes bootstrapped paths, survivorship bias, employee #5 vs #50 grant-math collapse, public-company RSU comp out-of-scope. Word count rose to 687 (above schema 250-500 guideline) but in line with the existing strong profiles (matt-levine 681, long-form-references 678, tax-and-finance 615) where mechanism-level specificity justifies length.

auto_review_history.reason: Additive config-load validation matching PR description; touches only src/blindspot/config.py and a new test file, no safety-sensitive paths, warn-only with no behavior change on valid configs.

summary: Added validate_safe_ranges() called from load_config(): emits UserWarning when any of 13 tunable knobs is outside the safe-range documented in config.yaml comments. Warn-only, no behavior change on valid configs.
rationale: PR #4 added safe-range comments to config.yaml but they lived only in human-readable form. This pass machine-enforces 13 dotted-path config keys (embedder.max_retries, tag_match.weights.{domain,entity,risk_surface,persona}, tag_match.{top_n,max_per_community}, normalization.embedding_similarity_threshold, critic.{grounding_pct_threshold,non_obviousness_min,max_regenerations}, cache.{fresh_ttl_days,evergreen_ttl_days}). Warn-only by design so experimental tuning isn't blocked. 3 unit tests pin the invariants. Strengthens the Config & scoring layer for future refine attempts.
next_suggested_path: With safe-ranges now machine-enforced, a future Config attempt could safely tune one knob (e.g., tag_match.weights.persona from 1.5 to 1.7) within its 1.0-2.0 safe-range and measure — assuming eval pipeline is unblocked by PR #11's JSON-parse hardening + further work.

auto_review_history.reason: Defensive hardening of JSON parser with new unit tests; touches only claude_agent_client.py + new test file, no safety/structural surfaces, rationale matches diff, and change is purely additive on happy path.

summary: Hardened _parse_json_response with one-shot last-} recovery and embedded-text ValueError; added 7 unit tests covering bare/fenced/prose-before/prose-after/both/unparseable/truncation cases.
rationale: Targets the JSONDecodeError-at-Community-Analyst diagnosis from PR #6 (run-20260517-0936). Two improvements: (1) one targeted recovery — re-decode the substring ending at the last } — handles trailing-prose responses raw_decode can't; (2) on unrecoverable failure, ValueError now embeds the full response text (truncated to 2000 chars), making the next eval failure diagnosable upstream. anthropic_api_client uses native tool-call JSON (no string-parse path), so no mirror change needed.
next_suggested_path: Next eval-layer subagent should run bin/blindspot eval (with VOYAGE_API_KEY exported) on a fresh main checkout; the new diagnostic ValueError will surface the actual failing response if Community Analyst hits a real malformed-JSON case. The reported hang ≥8min likely points at upstream (claude-agent-sdk auth or source-fetch); diagnose that next.

auto_review_history.reason: Scoped, additive eval-runner safety net: asyncio.wait_for around per-fixture pipeline+judge, new config knob within safe range (60-900), CLI flag, aggregate filters timed-out fixtures; touches no rejection-list paths and leaves blindspot ask unchanged.

summary: Added per-fixture timeout in eval runner (asyncio.wait_for, default 240s), new config knob eval.per_fixture_timeout_seconds:240 (safe-range 60-900), --per-fixture-timeout CLI flag, new result fields timed_out/timed_out_count/timed_out_ids
rationale: 5 consecutive runs with eval_status=pipeline-unavailable. PR #11 hardened JSON parsing; symptom persisted. This PR adds a safety net so eval CONTINUES on a hung fixture rather than dying silently — aggregate quality_score is computed over non-timed-out only, and timed_out_ids tells future eval-layer attempts exactly which fixtures hang. LLM client untouched; blindspot ask UX unchanged. Subagent committed locally (f6963ec) and pushed the branch but did not open the PR or return a verdict JSON — orchestrator salvaged by opening PR #15, running auto-review (approve iteration 1), and merging.
classification_of_previous: POSITIVE (4 merges last run, all iteration-1 approves, still no measured eval)
next_suggested_path: Try running ./bin/blindspot eval on main (orchestrator side, which has venv) to see if the per-fixture timeout lets at least some fixtures complete; if it does, capture the first measured quality_score since 2026-05-14 and treat as new baseline.
